steps.html: This is the area around downtown Syracuse and Syracuse University. The population of Syracuse city is 148,000. The population of its greater area including suburbs is 652,000.

This is Clay, New York, which has a population of 62,527. This spring, the chip manufacturer Micron is starting development on what will ultimately be a massive 1,400-acre complex featuring four individual fabrication buildings totaling approximately 7.2 million square feet. At full build-out by 2041, the site will house 2.4 million square feet of cleanroom space—roughly the size of 40 football fields—making it the largest semiconductor manufacturing facility in United States history.

The Micron megafab will draw its water from Lake Ontario, the 14th largest freshwater lake in the world, which was a primary reason the company chose the Clay site. To satisfy the plant's massive "thirst"—estimated at 48 million gallons per day at full build-out—infrastructure plans include a new 25-mile, 54-inch diameter pipeline running from a treatment plant in Oswego directly to the facility. This volume is staggering, as it exceeds the daily water usage of the entire city of Syracuse.

Micron Technology is based in Boise, Idaho. While it is now a global giant with facilities across the world, its origin story is a classic example of American "basement-to-billion-dollar-company" entrepreneurship.

While the Clay, NY, site will eventually be their largest, Micron already operates a massive global network of 13 manufacturing sites across North America and Asia. They generally divide their facilities into Front-End Fabs (where the actual silicon wafers are made) and Back-End Facilities (where those wafers are cut, tested, and packaged into final products).
